old_value: {"id":"project-LICHEN-2auf.35.2","title":"Align link parser policy and bounds","description":"Review of 2auf.35 found C rejects every encrypted frame while Python/Rust and spec/test-vectors/frame.json accept unsigned E=1 frames; C also rejects canonical maximum unsigned payloads above LICHEN_MAX_PAYLOAD. Align C/Python/Rust/spec/vectors on rejecting all E=1 frames, permit any payload whose complete frame fits the 255-byte body limit, and assert canonical error categories rather than any generic parse failure.","acceptance_criteria":"C/Python/Rust accept and reject the same positive/negative vectors; unsigned and signed E=1 both reject as unsupported encryption; canonical maximum unsigned frame serializes in C; negative vectors assert intended error categories; focused tests and three reviews pass.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-16T23:22:47.697331Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-16T23:22:47.697331Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"project-LICHEN-2auf.35.2","depends_on_id":"project-LICHEN-2auf.35","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-07-16T23:22:47.890118Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","metadata":"{}"}]}

new_value: {"notes":"Completed in clean integration worktree. Unified all E=1 handling as encryption_unsupported across C/Python/Rust/spec/vectors; added exact negative categories; made C frame sizing subtraction-first and overflow-safe; accepted 251-byte maximum and rejected 252/SIZE_MAX; expanded RX capacity to 251; rejected MIC selectors 2..7; corrected LLSec/LENGTH/empty-payload spec contradictions; added pre-allocation guards and mutation-resistant tests. Final gates: C frame 14/14; Python focused 64 passed, 7 expected skips, Ruff clean; Rust lichen-link 68 unit + 2 integration + 1 doctest, fmt/clippy clean. Final three closure reviews: no findings."}
Parser policy and bounds aligned and independently verified

comment: Recovery review found the closed work remains uncommitted and accepts LENGTH=255, producing a 256-byte frame that exceeds all supported LoRa PHY buffers.

new_value: Completed in b4ebb84af; C/Python/Rust bounds, vectors, and exact error categories aligned
